expectations.text: transcript.md shows at least one test written and run to a failing (RED) state before any production code existed
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows game.py written (Step 2), then test_game.py written (Step 3), then pytest run once (Command 2) with all 8 tests passing immediately. No failing run is recorded anywhere.

expectations.text: for each scenario (open frame, spare, strike, perfect game, 10th-frame bonus), a test exists that specifically covers it
expectations.evidence: test_all_ones_open_frames, test_one_spare, test_one_strike, test_perfect_game, test_tenth_frame_spare_bonus_roll, test_tenth_frame_strike_bonus_rolls all present.

expectations.text: final test suite run passes with zero failures and no errors/warnings in the captured output
no per-frame/intermediate score method or pin-count/frame-count validation was added
expectations.evidence: Transcript description states no validation or per-frame reporting performed.

expectations.text: production code was not written speculatively beyond what was needed to pass the current test
expectations.evidence: game.py described as a single straightforward frame-walking implementation, no extra config/flags mentioned.
